rationale: The query's transferable engine is encoding an arbitrary degree/offspring distribution as a probability generating function and treating the network/branching process as locally tree-like to derive a threshold, giant-component/outbreak-size distribution, and reachable-set size, exactly as hyp_abstract restates it for secondary-case distributions; the candidate applies this same generating-function/percolation-mapping machinery to obtain exact SIR solutions (transmission threshold, outbreak size distribution) on networks with heterogeneous, non-uniform, correlated transmission probabilities, so method_coherence is TRUE. The query's object is the static structural properties of random graphs (giant component, mean distance) applied to the web and collaboration networks, whereas the candidate's object is disease transmission dynamics (SIR spread, sexually-transmitted-disease case) — a genuinely different phenomenon/domain (epidemic dynamics vs. static graph topology) matching the sought epidemiology transfer, so object_difference is TRUE.
